news.summary: In Columbus Grove, Ohio, on April 17, 2025, United Bancshares, Inc. (OTCQX: UBOH) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.23 per share, reflecting a 4.5% growth. news.summary: XPO will hold its first quarter conference call on April 30, 2025, at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time. The company moves 18 billion pounds of freight per year, serving approximately 55,000 customers with 614 locations and 38,000 employees in North America and Europe. XPO's contact information includes Investor Contact Brian Scasserra at +1-617-607-6429 and Media Contact Cole Horton at +1-203-609-6004. You can access the conference call replay until May 30, 2025, by calling toll-free at 1-877-660-6853 or internationally at +1-201-612-7415, using the passcode 13753296. The webcast and results will be available on www.xpo.com.
news.headline: XPO to hold Q1 conference call on April 30, 2025, with results available on xpo.com. 18 billion pounds of freight/year moved in North America by XPO. Call replay available until May 30. XPO has 55,000 customers, 614 locations, 38,000 employees. Investor contact: Brian Scasserra +1-617-607-6429. Media contact: Cole Horton +1-203-609-6004.

news.summary: Nvini Group Limited (Nasdaq: NVNI) received notification from Nasdaq stating it is not in compliance with Listing Rule 5550(a)(2), needing a minimum bid price of $1 per share, and Listing Rule 5550(b)(2), needing a minimum Market Value of Listed Securities (MVLS) of $35 million. The company, a top acquirer of private SaaS B2B firms in Latin America, faces potential consequences due to the violation.
news.headline: Nvni Group (Nasdaq: NVNI) gets non-compliance notice from Nasdaq for not meeting $1 bid price and $35 million MVLS.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the global economy is projected to grow by 4% in 2022, following a 3.3% growth in 2021. The growth is driven by strong consumer spending and investments, with emerging markets leading the way. Meanwhile, inflation is expected to reach 5% this year, surpassing the target in many countries. The labor market is also showing signs of improvement, with unemployment rates dropping to 6% globally. However, various challenges such as supply chain disruptions and geopolitical tensions continue to pose risks to the economic recovery.Experts suggest that policymakers should remain vigilant and take appropriate measures to ensure sustainable growth in the coming years.
news.headline: Sachem Capital schedules Q1 2025 earnings release and call.

news.summary: SoundThinking has welcomed Burton Goldfield, the ex-President and CEO of TriNet, to its Board of Directors. With this new appointment, SoundThinking anticipates benefiting from Goldfield's wealth of experience in the industry, contributing to the company's growth and development moving forward.
news.headline: SoundThinking adds Burton Goldfield, ex TriNet President/CEO, to its Board of Directors.

The Company is dedicated to leading in the development and marketing of autoimmune disease treatments.

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is forecasted to grow by 5.6% in 2021, the fastest rate seen since 1973. This growth is attributed to the rapid recovery being seen in major economies and the deployment of COVID-19 vaccines. However, despite this positive outlook, there is a noticeable disparity in the recovery between advanced economies and developing countries. Advanced economies are estimated to grow by 5.4% in 2021, while developing economies are predicted to experience a growth rate of 6%. The report also highlights the importance of ensuring equitable access to vaccines worldwide to support a more balanced economic recovery.
news.headline: Former Credit Suisse CEO Thiam announces candidacy for President in Ivory Coast.

news.summary: BigBear.ai, a prominent AI technology provider in various sectors including defense, national security, travel, trade, and enterprise, disclosed that its first quarter earnings report will be released on May 1, 2025, around 4:15 pm Eastern Time. news.headline: BigBear.ai will report Q1 2025 results on May 1, 2025, at 4:15 pm ET. It's a leading AI tech provider for defense and more.

news.summary: Approximately 33% of young adults in the United States are currently facing mental health challenges. To shed light on this issue, ten exceptional young filmmakers have created original short films addressing mental illness. These films will be showcased at Village East by Angelika. The festival has been made possible through the kind sponsorship of the Laurie M....
news.headline: 10th Anniversary of Changing Minds Young Filmmaker Festival on May 17 in NYC showcases 10 filmmakers addressing mental illness.

news.summary: Monetary stimulus in China and Europe is impacting Bitcoin's price as investor focus shifts, with Bitcoin showing signs of decoupling from traditional markets. Despite the S&P 500 index dropping 5.7% in April, Bitcoin soared to $85,000, rebounding 14% from its crash to $74,400. Speculation indicates potential gains above $90,000 with various metrics supporting Bitcoin's decoupling. The weakening US dollar adds pressure on the Federal Reserve, with China increasing stimulus measures and the European Central Bank cutting interest rates for the seventh time, exacerbating concerns of a global economic slowdown. The US dollar's decline against major currencies prompts calls for lower interest rates, despite opposition from US President Trump. Bitcoin miners' long-term commitment remains strong, with an 8% increase in hashrate, possibly mitigating fears of a sell-off post-halving.
news.headline: Bitcoin price may hit $90K in April due to stimulus measures, decoupling from markets, weakening dollar, and increased miner commitment.

news.summary: InTest Corporation (NYSE American: INTT), a company providing test and process technology solutions worldwide for manufacturing and testing in key industries like semiconductor, automotive/EV, defense/aerospace, industrial, life sciences, and safety/security, revealed it will unveil its Q1 2025 financial results prior to the opening of financial markets on Friday, May 2, 2025. news.summary: In Los Angeles, the board of directors of KB Home (NYSE: KBH) has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $.25 per share on the Company’s common stock. This dividend will be payable on May 22, 2025, to stockholders of record on May 8, 2025. KB Home, one of the leading homebuilders in the United States, operates in 49 markets and has constructed nearly 700,000 quality homes in over 65 years. The company holds the top position as the customer-ranked national homebuilder.

news.summary: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. revealed a significant lease deal with Carolina Herrera, Ltd., a luxury fashion brand based in NYC and a Puig Brands subsidiary. This agreement emphasizes Carolina Herrera's continued involvement in nurturing the fashion landscape of the area. The move signifies the brand's commitment to the neighborhood, reinforcing its significant role in the industry.

news.summary: Littleton, Colorado - Rare Element Resources Ltd. (RER) has revealed a revised timeline for launching operations at its rare earth processing and separation demonstration facility in Upton, Wyoming. The plant, constructed by a consortium led by General Atomics, is primed to play a crucial role in further refining the Company's design and financial strategies. Trading under the ticker symbol REEMF on the OTCQB, this initiative marks a significant step forward for RER.
news.headline: Rare Element Resources updates timeline for rare earth processing in Wyoming; Demonstration Plant to gather data for future design and economics.

news.summary: Trane Technologies, a leading global climate innovator and listed on NYSE as TT, has been commended for its corporate transparency and climate performance by CDP. This prestigious recognition marks the third consecutive year that the company has made it to CDP's annual 'A List'. Out of the 22,400 companies evaluated for their climate initiatives, Trane Technologies stands out as one of the top 2%, securing an 'A' rating. CDP's thorough assessment process analyzes the scope of disclosures and actions taken by companies.
news.headline: Trane Technologies (NYSE:TT) earns 'A List' spot on CDP for environmental leadership, among 2% of 22,400 companies assessed.

news.summary: In Houston, Permianville Royalty Trust (NYSE: PVL) revealed the net profits interest calculation for April 2025. This calculation was based on the reported oil production in January 2025 and natural gas production in December 2024. It also factored in accrued costs from February 2025. The Trust noted a decrease in the cumulative outstanding net profits shortfall, which dropped from around $1.1 million in the previous period.
news.headline: Permianville Royalty Trust reports net profits interest calculation for April 2025 based on oil production in January and gas production in December 2024, with an outstanding shortfall of $1.1 million.

news.summary: Isabella Bank Corporation (OTCQX: ISBA) headquartered in Mt. Pleasant, Michigan, recently announced their financial results for the first quarter of 2025. The company reported a net income of $3.9 million, which translates to earnings of $0.53 per diluted share. This marks an increase from the first quarter of 2024, where the net income was $3.1 million, or $0.42 per diluted share. The company's performance showed a significant improvement year-over-year in terms of profitability.
news.headline: Isabella Bank Corp. Q1 2025 net income: $3.9M, $0.53 diluted EPS, up from $3.1M, $0.42 diluted EPS in Q1 2024.

news.summary: According to recent reports, the annual inflation rate in the country rose to 5.2% in the month of September, marking a significant increase from the previous month's rate of 4.9%. The core inflation rate, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, also saw a rise to 4.6% in September, up from 4.2% in the previous month. This spike in inflation has been attributed to various factors, including supply chain disruptions and increasing energy costs. Economists are closely monitoring the situation to assess the potential impact on consumer spending and overall economic growth. The government is expected to take measures to address the rising inflation rate and its potential consequences.
news.headline: Egypt's first rate cut since 2020 as inflation is tamed, marking a significant move for the economy.

news.summary: Today, Asana, Inc. (NYSE: ASAN) revealed the addition of Marc Boroditsky to its Board of Directors. Mr. Boroditsky, with experience in growing revenue organizations in high-growth, multi-billion dollar revenue SaaS companies, will play a vital role in Asana’s further growth. Asana's co-founder, Dustin Moskovitz, expressed his enthusiasm about the appointment.
news.headline: Asana appoints revenue leader Marc Boroditsky to board of directors to aid growth with expertise in scaling SaaS revenue.
